BBC One is sold by Sky and Virgin Media in UK
The BBC is to sell a package of TV channels, including flagship channel BBC One, through pay-TV providers Sky and Virgin Media in a major overhaul of its distribution deals.
The move, which has been agreed in principle, will see the BBC's channels, including BBC Two, CBBC, CBeebies and the BBC News channel, available as part of a standard package on Sky and Virgin Media for the first time.

BBC One is sold by the BBC Board to the UK Government for £975 million.
The BBC Board agrees to sell the licence fee-funded commercial arm, BBC One, to the UK Government for £975 million.
The decision is made to allow the BBC to focus on its public service remit and to raise funds for the licence fee settlement.
The sale is subject to regulatory approval from Ofcom and is expected to be completed by the end of the year.
